ORDER OF THE COURT CONTINUING MOTION TO COMPEL FURHTER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS JAMES NGUYEN AND MADISON NGUYEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEMAND - DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. FURTHER BRIEFING SCHEDULE IS SET, OPPOSITION TO BE FILED BY 1/11; REPLY TO BE FILED BY 1/18/13. FURTHER HEARING DATE IS SET FOR JANUARY 28, 2013. MINUTE ORDER SIGNED BY THE COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: KATHERINE HUIBONHOA, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-28-2013 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS JAMES NGUYEN AND MADISON NGUYEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET 1 IS GRANTED. EACH OF THE DOCUMENT REQUESTS ARE REASONABLE AND NARROWLY TAILORED TO ELICIT RELEVANT EVIDENCE. PRIVACY RIGHTS DO NOT SEEM TO BE IMPLICATED IN THAT MANY OF THE RESPONSES CAN BE IN THE FORM OF CANCELLED CHECKS. THE PARTIES MAY ENTER INTO A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER, BUT ARE NOT REQUIRED TO DO SO. IF DEFENDANTS WISH TO HAVE SUCH A STIPULATION THEY MUST SUBMIT A DRAFT TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WITHIN ONE WEEK (FEBRUARY 4, 2013). PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE ONE WEEK (FEBRUARY 11, 2013) TO RESPOND. GIVEN THE IMMINENT TRIAL DATE, DEFENDANTS MUST PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS BY FEBRUARY 13, 2013 WHETHER OR NOT A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER IS IN EFFECT. DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT THAT THE COMPLAINT ITSELF IS TIME BARRED IS NOT AT ISSUE HERE IN THIS DISCOVERY PROCEEDING. IT MUST BE RESOLVED, IF AT ALL, BY A SEPARATE MOTION OR AT TRIAL. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFFS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,560. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE DISCOVERY PRODUCED BY DEFENDANTS SHALL ONLY BE PRODUCED TO PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEYS AND WILL ONLY BE USED BY THE PARTIES FOR PURPOSES OF THE LITIGATION INCLUDING TRIAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: TOM COHEN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on plaintiffs' motion for an Order of Examination and Tracing.

Dept. 425, Judge: Sylvia Husing Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Joan Merten #6922.

Jeffrey Belote, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs James Nguyen (present) and Madison Nguyen.
Defendants Anthony Nguyen and Monique Nguyen present in pro per, were sworn and examined in Dept. 525. Examinations concluded at 12:30pm. Pending the escrow documents from Anthony Nguyen, the court ordered the matter continued to Aug. 6, 2013 at 10 a.m. in Dept. 206 for re-assignment if necessary for further examination. Mr. Belote will notify the court to take the matter off calendar, if he receives all the required documents and further examination is no longer necessary. Mr. Belote will prepare and submit a proposed judgment as requested via email to Judge Husing for review and signing.

Plaintiffs James Nguyen And Madison Nguyen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand
Off Calendar Jan-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WARREN NGUYEN OF NGUYEN & CREW, TEL. NO. 292-4623 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. FRANCISCO A. GUTTIERREZ, TEL. NO. 512-9865 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modifies its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Plaintiffs James Nguyen And Madison Nguyen's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand for Production of documents, set 1 is Granted. Each of the document requests are reasonable and narrowly tailored to elicit relevant evidence. Privacy rights do not seem to be implicated in that many of the responses can be in the form of cancelled checks. The parties may enter into a stipulated protective order, but are not required to do so. If defendants wish to have such a stipulation they must submit a draft to plaintiff's counsel within one week (February 4, 2013). Plaintiff shall have one week (February 11, 2013) to respond. Given the imminent trial date, defendants must produce responsive documents by February 13, 2013 whether or not a stipulated protective order is in effect. Defendants' argument that the complaint itself is time barred is not at issue here in this discovery proceeding. It must be resolved, if at all, by a separate motion or at trial. Sanctions are awarded to plaintiffs in the amount of $1,560. The court orders that the discovery produced by defendants shall only be produced to plaintiff's attorneys and will only be used by the parties for purposes of the litigation including trial. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Tom Cohen, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.
